messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.body_plain:
Hi Jennifer,

The package is still failing in *nebbiolo1.*

To confirm, .NET6 will not be installed in nebbiolo1, we will have to
update the version of .NET of the package if we want it to work or
nebbiolo1, right ?

Thank you,
Meriem

Le jeu. 26 d?c. 2024 ? 12:51, Jennifer Wokaty <Jennifer.Wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>
a ?crit :

> I did install .NET on another machine. According to the latest reports,
> only the Linux devel server nebbiolo1 is failing for rmspc.
>
> Jennifer Wokaty (they/them)
>
> Waldron Lab at CUNY SPH
> Bioconductor Core Team
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Meriem Bahda <meriembahda at gmail.com>
> *Sent:* Thursday, December 26, 2024 12:09 PM
> *To:* Jennifer Wokaty <Jennifer.Wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>
> *Cc:* bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>; Kern, Lori <
> Lori.Shepherd at roswellpark.org>
> *Subject:* Re: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple
> platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
>
> Thank you, Jennifer. We will look into updating the package with a newer
> version of .NET when we have a chance.
>
> I am still receiving emails about the package failing in other hosts. Will
> that be solved too in the meantime?
>
> Thank you,
> Meriem
>
> Le lun. 23 d?c. 2024 ? 18:14, Jennifer Wokaty <
> Jennifer.Wokaty at sph.cuny.edu> a ?crit :
>
> Hi,
>
> Looking at https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download/dotnet
> <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download/dotnet__;!!LRXxDv2l!Sljhs8ptYBWw-1a2n2QMdusKwxEqyDmRC2dJJgTm06qjXZfGYky21uS9979K_LhdR7UxJAoP8-1goEwqX3AAw2FksJ-FXtI$>,
> support for .NET 6.0 ended November 12, 2024. While .NET 6.0 is available
> in the Ubuntu backports repository, please consider updating rmspc to work
> with a currently supported version of .NET.
>
> In the meantime, I've installed .NET 6.0 on our release Linux server and
> left .NET 8.0 on our devel Linux server, which is available via the Ubuntu
> repository by default as you may want to consider as a replacement.
>
> Jennifer Wokaty (they/them)
>
> Waldron Lab at CUNY SPH
> Bioconductor Core Team
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Kern,
> Lori via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
> *Sent:* Tuesday, December 17, 2024 7:07 AM
> *To:* Meriem Bahda <meriembahda at gmail.com>; bioc-devel at r-project.org <
> bioc-devel at r-project.org>
> *Subject:* Re: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple
> platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
>
> * This email originates from a sender outside of CUNY. Verify the sender
> before replying or clicking on links and attachments. *
>
> Our system administrator is currently out of office. When they return we
> will look into this.
>
>
> Lori Shepherd - Kern
>
> Bioconductor Core Team
>
> Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center
>
> Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics
>
> Elm & Carlton Streets
>
> Buffalo, New York 14263

> Subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform
> build/check report for BioC 3.20
>
> Dear Community,
>
> I submitted a recent version of the package rmspc to Bioconductor a year
> ago, to upgrade it. A prerequisite for the rmspc package is .NET 6.0.
> Before installing the rmspc package, the program .NET 6.0. should already
> be installed on the user's computer.
>
> I recently received an email saying the package didn't pass the platform
> build check for the host *nebbiolo2*. I believe this error may have
> occurred because .NET 6.0 isn't installed on this platform.
>
> Thank you for your help!
>
> --
>
> *Meriem BAHDA*

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.body_plain:
Yes that is correct.  It is Bioconductor's policy that packages should work with the up-to-date versions of system dependencies to ensure security and maintenance. As Jennifer pointed out support for .NET 6.0 ended November 12, 2024.  You will need to update to work with .NET 8.0 on the devel nebbiolo1 machine.

Subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20

Dear Community,

I submitted a recent version of the package rmspc to Bioconductor a year
ago, to upgrade it. A prerequisite for the rmspc package is .NET 6.0.
Before installing the rmspc package, the program .NET 6.0. should already
be installed on the user's computer.

I recently received an email saying the package didn't pass the platform
build check for the host *nebbiolo2*. I believe this error may have
occurred because .NET 6.0 isn't installed on this platform.

Thank you for your help!

--

*Meriem BAHDA*

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Course, Kenya 2025: Applications Open!
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Course, Kenya 2025: Applications Open!
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Community,

We?re excited to announce our first in-person Bioconductor course in Kenya, taking place March 24?28, 2025, in Nairobi! This free, week-long course is tailored for bioinformatics beginners and builds on our global Carpentry program.

Participants will gain hands-on experience in R, Bioconductor, and RNA-seq workflows while supporting the growth of the Bioconductor community in Africa.


  *   Apply now<https://forms.gle/7ofqnEy6jD95dPV66>
  *   More details<https://training.bioconductor.org/workshops/2025-03-Nairobi/index.html>

If you know researchers in Africa who would benefit from this course, feel free to share this opportunity! For updates on future events, join the Bioconductor Africa mailing list<https://training.bioconductor.org/workshops/2025-03-Nairobi/index.html#??-join-the-bioconductor-africa-community>.

Best regards,
Maria

Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager

School of Medicine,
University of Limerick, Limerick, V94 T9PX
Ireland
Email: maria.doyle at ul.ie<mailto:maria.doyle at ul.ie>
Phone (office): +353 61 234 768
Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *   GBCC 2025 (June 23-26, New York, USA)<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/bioconductor_gbcc2025-bioconductor-galaxy-activity-7236739126190829569-QdEH>
  *   EuroBioC 2025 (September 17-19, Barcelona, Spain)<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/bioconductor_eurobioc2024-eurobioc2025-activity-7237888796564885505-hUW6>

I work flexible hours across several time zones. I don't expect you to read or respond to my emails outside of your normal working hours.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] spoon problems reported Multiple platform build/check BioC 3.21
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] spoon problems reported Multiple platform build/check BioC 3.21
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

Thanks for reading my question!

I received an email that my package has build errors, linked here:
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.21/bioc-LATEST/spoon/nebbiolo1-buildsrc.html

I haven't updated the package since the last build, so I'm not sure why I'm
getting the "memory not mapped" error now. I tested out the vignette on my
local computer and it still runs very quickly and does not use much memory
at all. How can I go about addressing this?

Thank you,
Kinnary Shah
